name: Pickled Bell Peppers
recipeInstructions:
Yield: About 9 pints
Procedure:
1. Wash the bell peppers and cut them into quarters, removing cores and seeds. Discard any blemishes.
2. Slice the peppers into strips.
3. Bring sugar, vinegar, and water to a boil for 1 minute in a large pot.
4. Add the pepper strips to the boiling mixture and return to a boil.
5. Pack half-pint jars with garlic cloves (1 whole) and canning salt (1/4 teaspoon), leaving 1/2-inch headspace.
6. Pack pint jars with double the amounts of garlic cloves and canning salt, again leaving 1/2-inch headspace.
7. Cover the jars with the hot vinegar mixture, sealing tightly.
8. Process half-pint jars in a boiling-water canner for 5 minutes at altitudes up to 1,000 feet; add 5 minutes for each additional 1,000 feet up to 6,000 feet; and add 10 minutes above 6,000 feet.
Note: Adjust lids as directed by the processing time.
